policy.title: Privacy Policy
policy.section1_desc: BarNote (hereinafter 'Company') processes personal information for the following purposes. The personal information being processed will not be used for purposes other than the following, and if the purpose of use is changed, prior consent will be obtained.
policy.s1_item1: Verification of membership registration intent and provision of member services
policy.s1_item2: Analysis of tasting notes and taste preferences, and provision of customized services
policy.section2_desc: The Company collects the following personal information to provide smooth services.
policy.s2_item1: Required items: Email, password, nickname, device information
policy.s2_item2: Optional items: Profile picture, short introduction
policy.s2_item3: Service usage records: Access logs, IP address, tasting notes, follow records, etc.
policy.section3_title: 3. Retention and Use Period of Personal Information
policy.section3_desc: In principle, after the purpose of processing personal information is achieved, the information is destroyed without delay. (However, if it is necessary to preserve it in accordance with relevant laws and regulations, it will be preserved for the period specified by the relevant laws and regulations.)
policy.section4_title: 4. Rights, Duties of Data Subjects and How to Exercise Them
policy.section4_desc: Users can view or modify their registered personal information at any time, and may request withdrawal of consent and cancellation of membership.
policy.section5_desc: For inquiries, complaints, or damage relief related to personal information protection, please contact us at the email address below.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.section1_desc: These Terms govern the rights, obligations, and responsibilities between BarNote (the "Company") and users in connection with the use of the BarNote service (the "Service") provided by the Company. These Terms also constitute the End User License Agreement (EULA) for the app and apply equally when you download or use the app through an app marketplace (Apple App Store, Google Play, etc.).
terms.section2_desc: The definitions of the key terms used in these Terms are as follows.
terms.s2_item1: "User" means a member who receives the Service provided by the Company in accordance with these Terms.
terms.s2_item2: "Member" means a person who registers for the Service and is able to use the Service provided by the Company.
terms.s2_item3: "Content" means the signs, text, figures, colors, voice, sound, and images (such as tasting notes and photos) that a member posts within the Service.
terms.section3_desc: Subject to your compliance with these Terms, the Company grants you a limited, non-transferable, non-exclusive, and revocable license to use the app on devices that you own or control. This license is limited to your personal, non-commercial use.
terms.s3_item1: You may not copy, distribute, rent, sell, or sublicense the app, or create derivative works of it.
terms.s3_item2: Except to the extent expressly permitted by applicable law, you may not reverse engineer, decompile, disassemble, or attempt to extract the source code of the app.
terms.s3_item3: You may not circumvent or disable any security features of the app, or interfere with the normal operation of the Service.
terms.s3_item4: All intellectual property rights in the app and the Service belong to the Company or its rightful owners, and the grant of this license does not constitute a transfer of any such rights.
terms.section4_title: Article 4 (Effect and Amendment of the Terms)
terms.section4_desc: The Company may amend these Terms within the scope that does not violate applicable laws when reasonable grounds arise. The amended Terms will be announced through notices within the Service and take effect from the date of such announcement.
terms.section5_desc: When using the Service, members must not engage in any of the following acts. A violation may result in restriction of Service use or deletion of the account.
terms.s5_item1: Stealing another person's information or entering false information
terms.s5_item2: Infringement of the intellectual property rights, including copyrights, of the Company or a third party
terms.s5_item3: Acts that damage the reputation of, or interfere with the business of, the Company or a third party
terms.s5_item4: Spam, distribution of malicious code, or other illegal or improper acts
terms.section6_desc: In providing a service in which users can freely write and share tasting notes, the Company applies a Zero Tolerance Policy toward objectionable content (Objectionable Content) and abusive users (Abusive Users).
terms.s6_item1: Definition of objectionable content: This includes hate speech, sexual or obscene content, content that harasses or threatens others, content that promotes violence, content that encourages illegal activity, and the like.
terms.s6_item2: Prior blocking and removal: If objectionable content is found or reported within the Service, the Company may immediately and completely delete or hide the relevant post (tasting note, profile, etc.) without prior notice.
terms.s6_item3: User sanctions: A user who posts objectionable content or behaves offensively toward others may have their account immediately blocked without warning, or be permanently restricted from using the Service.
terms.s6_item4: Reporting and response: Users may report objectionable content or users through the in-app reporting feature, and the Company will review the report immediately upon receipt and take appropriate corrective action within 24 hours.
terms.section7_title: Article 7 (Special Provisions for Use via App Marketplaces)
terms.section7_desc: If you download and use the app through a third-party app marketplace such as the Apple App Store or Google Play (the "App Marketplace"), the terms of service set by the relevant App Marketplace apply together with these Terms, and the following provisions additionally apply. In the event of a conflict between these Terms and the App Marketplace's terms, the App Marketplace's terms prevail.
terms.s7_item1: Parties: This EULA is concluded solely between the Company and the user, and the App Marketplace operators (Apple, Google, etc.) are not parties to these Terms. The Company is solely responsible for the app and its content.
terms.s7_item2: Scope of License: For apps provided through the Apple App Store, use is limited to use on Apple-branded devices that you own or control, in accordance with the Usage Rules set forth in Apple's App Store Terms of Service.
terms.s7_item3: Maintenance and Support: The Company is solely responsible for maintenance and customer support for the app, and the App Marketplace operators have no obligation whatsoever to furnish any maintenance or support services.
terms.s7_item4: Warranty: To the extent permitted by applicable law, the Company is solely responsible for any product warranties for the app. In the event of any failure of the app to conform to an applicable warranty, then, for apps purchased through the Apple App Store only, you may notify Apple and Apple may refund the purchase price of the app; to the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, Apple will have no other warranty obligation whatsoever.
terms.s7_item5: Product and Rights Claims: The Company, and not the App Marketplace operators, is responsible for addressing and handling any claims relating to the app, including product liability, legal or regulatory non-compliance, consumer protection, and intellectual property infringement.
terms.s7_item6: Legal Compliance: You represent and warrant that you are not located in a country that is subject to a U.S. Government embargo and that you are not listed on any U.S. Government list of prohibited or restricted parties.
terms.s7_item7: Third-Party Beneficiary: Apple and its subsidiaries are third-party beneficiaries of these Terms and, upon your acceptance of these Terms, will have the right to enforce these Terms against you.
terms.section8_desc: The Company shall not be liable for the provision of the Service where it is unable to provide the Service due to force majeure, such as natural disasters, Service maintenance or failures, or causes attributable to the user. Unless otherwise specifically provided by applicable law, the Company is not liable in connection with the use of the Service that is provided free of charge.
terms.section9_desc: Inquiries regarding the use of the Service, these Terms (EULA), or reports of objectionable content may be submitted to the contact below, and the Company will respond promptly in accordance with Article 6 upon receipt of a report.
terms.section10_desc: In the event of a dispute arising in connection with the use of the Service, the parties shall resolve it through agreement in accordance with the principle of good faith; if it is not amicably resolved, the matter may be brought before a competent court. The court having jurisdiction over the location of the Company's head office shall have exclusive jurisdiction.
